# Meeting Notes

This page documents relevant coaching sessions, internal team discussions, decisions and next steps during the development of the visualisation product. The notes are sorted in reverse chronological order.

## 31.05.2026

Topics:

* Final review of the project repository and current Quarto website structure.
* Review of the final dashboard prototype generated from `eda/generate-data-profile.py`.
* Discussion of where the final dashboard is stored and how it should be accessed.
* Review of the remaining documentation tasks, especially `viz_design_report.qmd`, `evaluation.qmd`, `meeting_notes.qmd` and `README.md`.

Decisions:

* The current dashboard design is considered suitable for the final submission.
* Design changes should only be made in `eda/generate-data-profile.py`; the generated `dashboard.html` should not be edited manually.
* The team will clarify whether the final dashboard should be linked directly in the Quarto website, for example as a link on the start page or as a separate navigation entry.
* The remaining work focuses mainly on documentation, consistency checks and final polishing.

Next steps:

* Review `viz_design_report.qmd` and `evaluation.qmd`.
* Update `meeting_notes.qmd` with relevant project decisions.
* Update `README.md` with project structure and usage instructions.
* Decide whether `dashboard.html` should be copied to `docs/eda/` and linked in the Quarto sidebar.

## 16.05.2026

Topics:

* Review of the Quarto website setup and page structure.
* Integration of the main project documents into the website navigation.
* Preparation of the presentation page and alignment with the project reports.

Decisions:

* The project documentation is maintained as a Quarto website.
* The sidebar navigation should include the main project pages: project overview, project charta, data report, visualisation design report, evaluation, meeting notes and presentation.
* Generated output folders such as `build` and `_freeze` should not be edited manually.

Next steps:

* Continue reviewing the content of the main `.qmd` files.
* Check whether all report pages are rendered correctly.
* Ensure that the presentation page is consistent with the final project story.

## 15.04.2026

Topics:

* Development of the first interactive visualisation prototype.
* Discussion of dashboard layout, visual encoding and interaction options.
* Selection of charts and dashboard components for the cybersecurity threat analysis.

Decisions:

* The visualisation product should be implemented as an interactive dashboard.
* The dashboard should combine overview KPIs, country-level information, attack type analysis, industry analysis, financial loss views, heatmaps and an incident table.
* Filters should allow users to explore the data by year, severity, attack type, country and attack source.
* The dashboard should support the needs of both analytical users and decision-makers.

Next steps:

* Implement the dashboard prototype.
* Align the visualisation choices with the design rationale in `viz_design_report.qmd`.
* Test whether the dashboard supports the main project questions.

## 02.04.2026

Topics:

* Review of the cybersecurity dataset and exploratory data analysis.
* Discussion of relevant variables for the visualisation product.
* Preparation of the data report and profiling output.

Decisions:

* The project uses the Global Cybersecurity Threats 2015–2024 dataset.
* The main analysis focuses on countries, years, attack types, target industries, attack sources, vulnerabilities, defence mechanisms, financial losses, affected users and incident resolution time.
* A profiling report is generated to document the structure and quality of the dataset.
* The data report should explain the dataset, key variables, data quality and initial findings.

Next steps:

* Complete the data report.
* Generate and link the interactive profiling report.
* Use the EDA findings as basis for the visualisation design.

## 20.03.2026

Topics:

* Definition of the project scope and initial research direction.
* Discussion of the target users and value proposition.
* Initial planning of the visualisation product and project documentation.

Decisions:

* The project focuses on global cybersecurity threats between 2015 and 2024.
* The main goal is to make cybersecurity incidents easier to explore and compare across countries, industries, attack types and impact dimensions.
* The visualisation product should support both strategic overview and detailed incident exploration.
* The main target users are analysts and security decision-makers.

Next steps:

* Finalise the project charta.
* Define the guiding research questions.
* Start data acquisition and initial data exploration.
* Prepare the structure of the Quarto project website.
